channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: GE Proposes New Clean-sheet High-Thrust Engine for AMCA Mk2 with F-414 Footprint
item.description: India’s next-generation fighter engine roadmap under the AMCA programme has entered a decisive phase, with GE Aerospace reportedly proposing a significant shift in approach. According to sources, GE has now offered a “clean-sheet” or heavily enhanced engine derived from its existing core technology, specifically optimised for the Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) Mk2’s stealth architecture and 25-tonne class airframe.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAF-DRDO Study Aims to Turn Mirage 2000 into Strategic Interceptor
item.description: India is evaluating a major capability upgrade for its Mirage 2000 (Vajra) fleet, with idrw.org being told that the Indian Air Force, in collaboration with DRDO, is conducting a high-priority feasibility study to integrate the Astra Mk2 beyond-visual-range air-to-air missile onto the platform. The move is aimed at transforming the Mirage 2000 from a reliable interceptor into a long-range “sniper” capable of engaging targets well beyond traditional engagement envelopes.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Medium Transport Aircraft Deal to Mandate 60% Indigenous Content, ALG Operations and Local Production Push
item.description: India is preparing to launch one of its most significant airlift modernization programs as the Indian Air Force (IAF) moves forward with a mega tender for 60 Medium Transport Aircraft (MTA) to replace its aging An-32 fleet. Sources indicate that the upcoming procurement will not just be a platform acquisition but a strategic push to reshape India’s military aviation ecosystem through indigenous manufacturing, localized sustainment, and high-altitude operational capability.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India–US F414 Engine Deal Nears Finalisation as GE-HAL Conclude Critical Technology Talks
item.description: According to report in Economic times, India and the United States have achieved a major breakthrough in their landmark fighter jet engine co-production programme, with GE Aerospace and Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) concluding the most complex phase of technical negotiations for the F414 engine. The development marks a decisive step toward finalising a contract later this year, a move expected to significantly strengthen the Indian Air Force’s (IAF) combat capabilities amid declining squadron strength.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s ET-LDHCM Hypersonic Missile Advances Under Project Vishnu with Booster Integration Progress
item.description: India’s ambitious hypersonic strike programme under Project Vishnu is entering a critical development phase, with the fabrication of specialized jigs and fixtures for booster integration of the Extended Trajectory Long Duration Hypersonic Cruise Missile (ET-LDHCM) now underway. This seemingly industrial milestone carries major strategic significance, as such tooling systems are essential for ensuring precision alignment, structural integrity, and repeatability during the integration of complex hypersonic propulsion stacks.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: US Pledges Seamless Induction of MQ-9B Predator Drones into IAF as Deliveries Begin from 2029
item.description: In a significant boost to India’s long-endurance surveillance and precision strike capabilities, the United States has reaffirmed its commitment to ensure the seamless induction of MQ-9B Predator drones into the Indian Armed Forces, with deliveries expected to commence from January 2029. The assurance comes amid deepening defence cooperation between New Delhi and Washington, particularly in the domain of high-end unmanned systems and network-centric warfare.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Weighs European 6th-Gen Fighter Programs as Russia Offers Su-57/Su-75 Upgrade Path
item.description: India is entering a phase in its future air combat strategy, as senior defence sources indicate that the Indian Air Force (IAF) and Ministry of Defence (MoD) are actively evaluating pathways to access sixth-generation fighter technologies. According to sources, Russia has proposed an alternative approach—offering the Su-57 Felon and Su-75 Checkmate as evolving platforms that can be progressively upgraded into “6th-generation-like” systems through joint development.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Rejects China’s Attempt To Rename Places, Reaffirms Arunachal Pradesh An ‘Integral And Inalienable Part’
item.description: India on Sunday rejected China’s “mischievous attempts” to assign “fictitious names” to places which are part of the country, reiterating that territories, including Arunachal Pradesh, remain an “integral and inalienable part” of it.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India, China boosts LPG imports from US to replace West Asia supply
item.description: Asia’s biggest liquefied petroleum gas LPG importers, including India and China, are racing to replace disrupted West Asia supplies with cargoes from the Americas, driving spot premiums to record highs, analysts and traders said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Foreign Secretary Misri Arrives In Paris For India-France Foreign Office Consultations
item.description: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri arrived in Paris on Sunday for the India–France Foreign Office Consultations. In a statement on X, the Indian Embassy in France stated: “Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri arrives in Paris for the India–France Foreign Office consultations. The visit follows French President H.E Emmanuel Macron’s visit to India in February 2026 and reflects the continued regular high-level exchanges between India and Europe.”
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Talks ended without deal due to ‘excessive demands’ made by US: Iran
item.description: The talks between Iran and the US in Pakistan have ended without a deal due to “excessive demands” made by the American side, a top Iranian official said on Sunday. Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei, however, emphasised that “diplomacy never ends.” US Vice President JD Vance, who led the American delegation, said the talks failed to reach a peace deal, citing Tehran not forgoing its nuclear programme as one of the key sticking points. He said the American side presented its “final and best offer” to the Iranian side, but it did not accept it.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: From fake PAN cards to forged passports: JK police unravels LeT’s pan-India logistical web
item.description: Widening its crackdown on the inter-state network of the Lashkar-e-Taiba terror group, the Jammu and Kashmir Police, in coordination with central intelligence agencies, has detained several people from Haryana and Rajasthan for allegedly facilitating the acquisition of fraudulent identities, including passports, by terrorists.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: All Indian Seafarers In Gulf And West Asia Region Safe, Over 2,084 Repatriated: Centre
item.description: All Indian seafarers in the Gulf and West Asia region are safe and no incident involving Indian-flagged vessels has been reported in the past 24 hours, the government said on Sunday. The Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways, through the Directorate General of Shipping (DG Shipping), has facilitated the safe repatriation of more than 2,084 Indian seafarers so far, including 75 in the last 24 hours from various locations across the Gulf region.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Territorial Army, Assam Rifles inspire NCC cadets with advanced drone technology in Mizoram
item.description: In a significant initiative aimed at inspiring and educating the next generation of leaders, the Territorial Army, in collaboration with a drone lab, conducted an engaging lecture-cum-demonstration for young NCC students in Mizoram, officials said on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: MoS Margherita attends mourning ceremony for late Iranian leader Ayatollah Khamenei
item.description: Minister of State for External Affairs Pabitra Margherita on Sunday attended a mourning ceremony for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. The ceremony was organised by the Iranian embassy to pay respect to the departed leader on the 40th day (Chehelum) of his demise.
item.dc:creator: admin
